channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania urges content creators to promote the country through AFCON 2027
item.description: Digital content creators in Arusha have been called upon to take advantage of the upcoming Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON 2027) tournament, which will be hosted in Tanzania, to produce content that will...

item.title: Tanzania marks Health Day urging investment in scientific research for stronger health systems
item.description: Tanzania, alongside the World Health Organization (WHO), the National Institute for Medical Research (NIMR), and CHIEB partners, celebrated World Health Day with a strong call to action for African co...

item.title: US and Iran agree to two-week ceasefire, causing a dip in oil prices and a boost in US stock futures
item.description: In a significant diplomatic development, the United States and Iran have agreed to a two-week ceasefire, easing tensions that have contributed to the volatility of global oil prices. The ceasefire agr...

item.title: DOE Approves Petroleum and Natural Hydrogen Projects in Visayas
item.description: The Department of Energy (DOE) of the Philippines announced that President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has approved two new petroleum service contracts in the Visayas region. The first, Service Contract No. ...

item.title: PSX Benchmark KSE-100 Surges Record 13,794 Points on US-Iran Ceasefire Announcement
item.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange witnessed a historic intraday rally on Wednesday as the benchmark KSE-100 index surged by over 13,000 points following positive news of a ceasefire agreement between the Un...

item.title: Ex-Kano Governor Kabiru Gaya resigns from APC after wide consultation
item.description: Former Kano State governor and ex-senator Kabiru Gaya has officially left the All Progressives Congress (APC), deepening internal problems in the party’s Kano State chapter. In a resignation letter da...

item.title: Matthew Perry's Family Seeks Severe Sentence in Actor's Fatal Ketamine Case
item.description: Matthew Perry’s family has publicly urged the court to impose the maximum possible prison sentence on the woman accused of supplying the ketamine allegedly linked to the late actor’s death. The case c...

item.title: UK set for a warm spell before temperatures drop
item.description: The UK is expecting a brief warm spell, with temperatures reaching up to 26°C in some areas. This rise will bring sunshine and warmer conditions, offering a welcome change from the recent cooler weath...

item.title: New Research Unveils Complex Communication and Intelligence in Plant Life
item.description: Recent scientific discoveries are revealing that plants possess a level of intelligence and communication previously unimaginable. Far from being passive organisms, plants actively perceive their envi...

item.title: Joseph Kabila Declared Fugitive in DR Congo After Treason Conviction
item.description: Former President of the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Joseph Kabila, has been declared a fugitive by Congolese authorities following a treason conviction. A military court found him guilty in abse...

item.title: Government Initiates Borehole Projects to Provide Clean Water in Kisumu West
item.description: Residents of Kisumu West can look forward to safer and more reliable water access within the next three months following the launch of new borehole projects by the National Water Harvesting and Storag...

item.title: Practical Guide to Organizing a Successful First Corporate Concert
item.description: This Moss and Fog article offers a straightforward guide to planning a successful corporate concert, emphasizing the importance of preparation and attention to detail. It begins by noting how concerts...

item.title: Delhi HC Seeks Action Over Journalist Rana Ayyub's Posts on Hindu Deities and Savarkar
item.description: The Delhi High Court has called for urgent action against journalist Rana Ayyub regarding her posts on the social media platform X (formerly Twitter) between 2013 and 2017. The court's decision comes ...

item.title: Kruger National Park visitor fined for endangering himself and others during lion sighting
item.description: A tourist at Kruger National Park in South Africa was fined R2,500 after disregarding park safety rules and stepping out of his vehicle to take photos of lions. Other visitors warned him of the danger...

item.title: Government boosts medical tourism to strengthen health sector and economy
item.description: Dodoma — Tanzania government dey push hard for medical tourism as part of efforts to grow health sector contribution to the national economy. Deputy Minister for Health, Florence Samizi, talk today du...

item.title: David Raya shines as Arsenal secure narrow win over Sporting in Champions League quarter-final first leg
item.description: Arsenal managed a hard-fought 1-0 victory against Sporting in the first leg of their Champions League quarter-final at Estádio José Alvalade. The breakthrough came late in the match when Kai Havertz, ...

item.title: National Green Tribunal orders halt on tree cutting in Nashik ahead of Simhastha Kumbh Mela
item.description: The Pune bench of the National Green Tribunal (NGT) has issued an interim stay on tree felling in Nashik in connection with preparations for the Simhastha Kumbh Mela, effective until April 28. This ac...

item.title: Montreal Alouettes re-sign top tackler Micah Awe for 2026 season
item.description: The Montreal Alouettes have secured the return of Micah Awe, their star linebacker, for the 2026 season. Awe, who was pivotal to the Alouettes' defense last year, has signed a one-year contract extens...

item.title: Challenges Facing Kenya's Social Health Authority in Collecting Premiums
item.description: Kenya's Social Health Authority (SHA) is facing serious challenges in sustaining its operations due to a significant number of Kenyans not paying their premiums. Despite registering over 29 million in...

item.title: Government Moves to Regulate Knoflokskraal Settlement in Grabouw
item.description: The South African Department of Public Works is preparing to approach the Western Cape High Court to expand a 2021 containment order at Knoflokskraal in Grabouw. The settlement, started in 2020 during...
